MASTER MOTION CALENDAR ON DEC-23-2021 IN 206, DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO CONTINUE TRIAL DATE. JEFFREY P. WOO, ESQ. APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF VIA COURTCALL. ALLISON STRULL, ESQ. APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT VIA COURTCALL. THE COURT GRANTS DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO CONTINIUE TRIAL DATE. THE COURT GRANTS DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO CONTINUE TRIAL DATE. THE TRIAL DATE OF 3/7/22 IS CONTINUED TO 5/31/22 AT 9:30AM IN DEPT. 206. DISCOVERY TRACKS THE ORIGINAL TRIAL DATE OF 3/7/22. THE COURT DID NOT RECEIVE COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOTION WHICH IS IN VIOLATION OF SF LOCAL RULE 2.7B. THE COURT ORDERS DEFENDANT TO PAY $60.00 FILING FEE FOR THE CONTINUANCE WHICH IS THE COST FOR FILING PAPERS THAT DEFENDANT WOULD HAVE TO FILE AGAIN WITH A COURTESY COPY IN ORDER TO GET THE 5/31/22 TRIAL DATE. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER THAT IS APPROVED AS TO FORM. PARTIES WAIVED NOTICE. JUDGE: SAMUEL K. FENG; CLERK: R. VELUZ; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (206)

MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CUD-21-668139 WITH CASE CGC-21-590086
Off Calendar Dec-23-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES (via Zoom):

Raymond M. Yetka; Utrecht & Lenvin LLP; 415-357-0600; appearing on behalf of defendant Eat Just, Inc.
Jaimie Bombard; Cooper White & Cooper LLP; 415-433-1900; appearing on behalf of plaintiff 2000 Folsom Partners LLC.

Not contested. The court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT EAT JUST, INC.'S MOTION TO SOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CUD-21-668139 WITH CASE CGC-21-590086 is DENIED. Unlawful detainer actions are summary proceedings not appropriate for consolidation, except for in limited circumstances not applicable here.

Order signed. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

Motion For Protective Order
Off Calendar Dec-23-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES (via Zoom):

Raymond M. Yetka; Utrecht & Lenvin LLP; 415-357-0600; appearing on behalf of defendant Eat Just, Inc.
Jaimie Bombard; Cooper White & Cooper LLP; 415-433-1900; appearing on behalf of plaintiff 2000 Folsom Partners LLC.

Plaintiff's counsel contests the court's tentative ruling. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER is GRANTED. Complete verified responses shall be provided no later than (1) January 20, 2022 if motion to continue trial is denied; or (2) February 15, 2022 if motion to continue trial is granted.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

Defendant'S Notice Of Motion For Protective Order
Off Calendar Dec-13-2021 Off Calendar

Defendant Eat Just, Inc.'S Notice Of Motion For Protective Order
Off Calendar Aug-04-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. JAIMIE BOMBARD, Esq. of Cooper, White & Cooper LLP (Tel: 415-433-1900) for plaintiff;
Atty. RAYMOND M. YETKA, Esq. of Utrecht & Lenvin, LLP (Tel: 415-357-0600) for defendant Eat Just, Inc.

Hearing Re: Defendant Eat Just, Inc.'s Motion For Protective Order.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The Judge Pro Tem and counsel for both sides (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling with the sole modification of granting the motion without prejudice, as follows:

The Motion for a Protective Order is granted, defendant does not have to answer plaintiff's Special Interrogatories (Set One) served May 14, 2021. The 2 interrogatories at issue, are based on San Francisco's Commercial Eviction Moratorium's language as quoted verbatim, but this case has no eviction cause of action. This case is for alleged breach of contract and unpaid rent. There was no showing that gross receipts as defined, is relevant to a claim for unpaid rent. Likewise, there was no showing that the number of full-time employees of the defendant is relevant to a claim for unpaid rent.

No sanctions are awarded.

Motion is granted without prejudice to the parties' rights to conduct further discovery as is allowed by code.

The moving party is to prepare a proposed order in editable MS Word format and e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at maulawsf@hotmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Michael Mau; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
